parameters.content:
# Setup Checklist

### 1.Go to the Airtable Template and copy the latest version of the base
### 
### 2. From your new Airtable base URL, get and replace your base and tables id's into this workflow's trigger node.
### 3. Input your Airtable Id's in the second node "Airtable Base ID's"
### 4. Make sure to add a Personal Access Token for Airtable Integration. It should, as minimum have enabled scopes for "data.record:read", "data.record:write", "schema.bases:read"
### 5. Any file uploads can now be done from the Interface Form

#After Setup

### - Make sure you that if you add, remove or modify fields (or field names), those changes should also be applied to the "Create Record" node
### - Make sure that the CSV upload header row, matches the Airtable Leads field names
### - If you modify any field type (Text to Number, or Number to Text), those changes should also be applied to the "Create Records" value (Numbers go without double quotes / strings, dates and the rest of the data types go with double quotes) [JSON Syntax]

Make sure that the fields have the correct data type (Strings, Integers (numbers), etc)
### Input your Airtables relevant ID's. These will be used in the API Calls

# Setup Checklist

### 1.Go to the Airtable Template and copy the latest version of the base
### 
### 2. From your new Airtable base URL, get and replace your base and tables id's into this workflow's trigger node.
### 3. Input your Airtable Id's in the second node "Airtable Base ID's"
### 4. Make sure to add a Personal Access Token for Airtable Integration. It should, as minimum have enabled scopes for "data.record:read", "data.record:write", "schema.bases:read"
### 5. Any file uploads can now be done from the Interface Form

#After Setup

### - Make sure you that if you add, remove or modify fields (or field names), those changes should also be applied to the "Create Record" node
### - Make sure that the CSV upload header row, matches the Airtable Leads field names
### - If you modify any field type (Text to Number, or Number to Text), those changes should also be applied to the "Create Records" value (Numbers go without double quotes / strings, dates and the rest of the data types go with double quotes) [JSON Syntax]

prerequisites: An active n8n instance installed locally, in a Docker container, or hosted on n8n Cloud.
Basic familiarity with importing JSON templates in n8n.
setupInstructions: Download the JSON configuration for this workflow.
Ensure you set up credentials for all connected integrations.
Test your trigger and run execution.
requiredCredentials: airtable Token  A P I details
configurationSteps: Verify the settings of the trigger node "New Upload".
Open the "Get File ID" node (n8n-nodes-base.airtable) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Status Failed" node (n8n-nodes-base.httpRequest) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Status Uploaded" node (n8n-nodes-base.httpRequest) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note1" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Campaign is Not Empty" node (n8n-nodes-base.if) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Read File" node (n8n-nodes-base.spreadsheetFile) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Status Processing" node (n8n-nodes-base.httpRequest) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Download File" node (n8n-nodes-base.httpRequest) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Create Records" node (n8n-nodes-base.httpRequest) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note2" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note3" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note4" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Locate the Set/Edit fields node "Campaign Not Empty".
Define or edit values for variables that are referenced in downstream nodes.
Locate the Set/Edit fields node "Campaign Not Empty1".
Define or edit values for variables that are referenced in downstream nodes.
Locate the Set/Edit fields node "Airtable Base IDs".
Define or edit values for variables that are referenced in downstream nodes.
testingSteps: Click the "Test step" or "Listen for test event" button on your Trigger node.
Send a test trigger from your source service (e.g. fill a test form or trigger a webhook trigger).
Verify that the node canvas lights up green, indicating successful data flow.
Open the final destination node to confirm that output records were saved correctly.
instructions.expectedResult: Upon trigger activation, inputs will flow through the configured nodes and execute actions successfully.
troubleshooting: Check credential warning symbols. Most workflow issues stem from expired OAuth connections or missing API permissions.
Verify that the data keys coming from your Trigger node match the exact names mapped in subsequent nodes (e.g., {{ $json.body.email }}).
Open the Execution history tab in n8n to view logs and determine which node failed.
securityNotes: Never hardcode API keys, passwords, or authentication bearer tokens in the plain text fields of node parameters.
If you download or share this workflow JSON, sanitize any sensitive parameters or configuration nodes.
customizationIdeas: Add an email node at the end to notify you of successful executions.
Incorporate an AI LLM node to summarize trigger payloads before writing them to integrations.
